Title: Migrate from Payment API v2 to Payment API v3
Content:
Who should read this guide

This guide is intended for merchants who are currently using Payments API v2 (legacy) and want to migrate existing payment flows to Payments API v3.

Payments API v3 is the next-generation payments platform designed to simplify integrations, unlock new payment capabilities, and provide a more consistent, predictable and scalable experience for merchants.

Key Benefits

By migrating to v3, you will benefit from:

1. Clear, flow-Based payment flows: Payments API v3 introduces explicit payment types such as PAY, PAY_AND_SAVE, REUSABLE_PAYMENT_CODE, and independent Payment Token lifecycle. This makes payment flow clearer, and more predictable compared to v2, where flows were implicitly inferred from request structure.

2. Unified and future-Proof API design: v3 follows a standardized, RESTful design with consistent naming, lifecycle states, and object structure. This reduces edge cases and makes it easier to adopt new payment methods and features as they become available.

3. Better support for saved payment methods: tokenization and saved payment flows are first-class citizens in v3. Merchants can easily collect, store, and reuse payment token using a unified API, enabling subscriptions, one-click checkout, and recurring payments.

4. Richer payment lifecycle & webhooks: v3 provides a more structured payment lifecycle with standardized statuses and webhook events. This enables better monitoring, reconciliation, and error handling across different payment channels.

5. Improved multi-country & multi-channel scalability: with explicit country, channel_code, and channel_properties, v3 makes it easier to scale your integration across markets and payment methods without redesigning your core payment logic.

Important: Payments API v2 is considered legacy. New features and payment methods will only be introduced in Payments API v3.

High-level differences between v2 and v3

Area

Payments API v2

Payments API v3

API spec

Documented here

Documented here

Flow definition

Implicit. For example, including a payment_method object inside the payment_request body is treated as a pay-and-save flow.

Explicit via type, with supported type as

  • PAY

  • PAY_AND_SAVE

  • REUSABLE_PAYMENT_CODE

  • or Create Payment Token to Save payment method

Payment channel routing

Defined inside a payment group object with channel code and properties

Move away from unstandardized payment method group. Defined using channel_code and channel_properties at the top level of the request. Channel behavior and required fields are clearly defined and fully documented in the API reference.

Status & webhooks

Less standardized, with inconsistent event structures depending on payment method and flow.

Object- and flow-oriented webhook events with standardized lifecycle statuses for payment_request , payment , and payment_token objects.

How to migrate

Update your integration

Endpoints

Feature

Payment API v2

Payment API v3

API Endpoint

/payment_requests

/payment_methods

/v3/payment_requests

/v3/payment_tokens

Parameter Conversion

The new Payment API v3 supports all the functionality of the legacy Payment API v2. The following table maps the parameters and configuration between two APIs. Please refer here for the full lists.

Payment API v2

Payment API v3

Notes

reference_id

reference_id

A reference to uniquely identify your transaction/order.

-

type

Flow definition. Payment API v3 supports:

  • PAY (one time payment)

  • PAY_AND_SAVE (

  • REUSABLE_PAYMENT_CODE
